contract_red_flags.source: Claude chat: 'Charlotte rental investment submarkets and builder contracts' (May 10)
items: PRICE LOCK: push for a hard lock — no escalation clauses tied to material costs
RENTAL RESTRICTIONS (critical for leaseback/investor): read HOA CC&Rs for 1–2 yr rental bans or non-owner-occupancy caps BEFORE signing
WARRANTY ASSIGNMENT: confirm transferable to subsequent owners; do the 11-month walk before the 1-yr warranty expires (model sales: you may not get the 1-yr at all)
CLOSING-DATE FLEXIBILITY: builders can slip 6–12 months; negotiate a per-diem credit for missed close dates
PREFERRED-LENDER LOCK-IN: incentives are real but get a competing quote; 2-1 rate buydowns are currently aggressive and often worth taking
LOT SELECTION: interior/cul-de-sac lots hold value; avoid power lines, busy roads, corner-to-arterial
CHANGE ORDERS: for a rental keep upgrades minimal — LVP throughout, no carpet, upgraded laundry; skip premium finishes that don't move rent

rows.note: Highest appreciation; permits +400% since Blue Line ext.; your One NoDa Park tour fits here
30% of metro absorption; sub-$450K new TH rare
Pipeline collapsed 4,000 → 800 units; institutional money present
Family renters, low turnover; Eastwood & Taylor Morrison active
Buy near proposed stations before the premium prices in

key_insights.method: Cross-source triangulation: inbox threads × Drive research docs × comparison dashboard × 2027 upside rankings × market model × tour archive

insights.title: Your alpha is the incentive package, not the asset
insights.insight: Your own Craig Ave underwriting proves it: 8.5%/yr without the package vs 15.5%/yr with it. The $24,946 incentive + ~$10K furniture + $31,200 leaseback is nearly the entire excess return. Appreciation alone (2–4% metro base) barely beats carry.
insights.action: Only write offers with a ≥5%-of-price package. Hunt quarter-ends — Taylor Shane's flexibility appeared in writing on Jun 22, eight days before quarter close. Next window: Sep 22–30.
insights.sources: Craig Ave Investment Analysis · Archer Row thread · comparison dashboard

insights.title: One zone triangulates across every source: West Charlotte 28216/28208
insights.insight: Your 2027 upside pick #2 (West Blvd corridor, 8.5/10) + the metro's cheapest new townhome entry (Ryan Homes Sunset Creek from $284,990, 28216) + Corridors of Opportunity $259M + your own market model's best in-city appreciation forecast (6.5% YoY West CLT) all point at the same place. No other zone gets four independent confirmations.
insights.action: Tour Sunset Creek and the Lennar Sterling Pointe model (28216, furnished+leaseback lead, invest score 81). This corridor is the strongest new-money candidate in your data.
insights.sources: 2027 upside doc · Ryan Homes communities doc · market model · Corridors of Opportunity

insights.title: Cherry Grove violates your own avoid rule
insights.insight: The $269,990 Kannapolis Juniper scores 78 in-app on raw value, but your 2027 doc explicitly warns against 'far-out new construction with many remaining builder lots' — at resale, your buyer will comparison-shop against the builder's next incentive package. Great price ≠ great investment under your own framework.
insights.action: Treat Cherry Grove as a long-hold cash-flow play only, or pass. Don't let the price chip distract from the corridor plays.
insights.sources: 2027 upside doc (avoid list) · Cherry Grove inbox thread

insights.title: The Tri Pointe paradox: negotiate motivated, contract paranoid
insights.insight: Tri Pointe gives you the most model-sale supply (3 live threads: Archer Row, Southbridge, Oakhurst) and the only written 'name your scenario' invitation — but also your research's worst local execution signal (1.15/5 Southbridge reviews) and a model-warranty haircut (no 1-yr walk).
insights.action: Press hard on price/leaseback/furniture AND require: independent inspection, written warranty terms, CC&R rental-restriction check (your red-flags list), and the 11-month-walk equivalent in the contract.
insights.sources: True Homes and Tripointe doc · Archer Row thread · contract red-flags chat

insights.title: SC leaseback carries a hidden ~$3–4K/yr tax penalty
insights.insight: A leaseback means you don't occupy — and SC assesses non-owner-occupied at a 6% ratio: on a $400K Fort Mill townhome that's roughly $6,000/yr vs ~$2,400 owner-occupied. It quietly eats ~1.5 months of leaseback rent every year. Notably, ALL EIGHT of your 2027 upside picks are NC.
insights.action: Run leaseback holds in NC. Consider SC (Fort Mill/Indian Land) only for school-premium exit plays where you'll occupy or flip fast.
insights.sources: Tax model · Southbridge thread · 2027 upside doc

insights.title: Model homes clear in weeks — pre-stage, don't deliberate
insights.insight: Southbridge's York model went under contract within ~4 weeks of your visit; Oakhurst's HS78 fall-through re-sold; Archer Row's model released and got 'Home of the Week' billing days later. The window between a model release (or fall-through) and contract is shorter than a deliberation cycle.
insights.action: Keep offers pre-staged per builder (price + terms + contingencies ready). The daily scan is your first-mover edge — when it flags a release or fall-through, call the same day.
insights.sources: Southbridge thread · Oakhurst thread · Archer Row thread

insights.title: Craig Ave is the template — you already beat your own threshold
insights.insight: 86/100 by your scoring, $373,970 effective (15% under the $438K Oakhurst average) with the Cotswold halo ($707K) one neighborhood east and the Eastland Yards catalyst (your #1 upside pick) on the other side — a double-catalyst position bought below basis threshold.
insights.action: Replicate the pattern, not the address: model + leaseback + ≥5% incentive + a submarket with a documented value gap and a visible catalyst. DW's second model (Jon Wian) is the nearest repeat.
insights.sources: Comparison dashboard · Oakhurst/Cotswold thesis · 2027 upside doc

programs.name: Opendoor Exclusives
programs.what: Private off-MLS marketplace: 14-day exclusive listings before MLS. For BUYERS: appraisal-gap coverage up to $50K and pre-MLS access to homes at committed prices — a genuine early-look edge on Charlotte resale townhomes.
programs.action: Browse the Exclusives inventory for Charlotte weekly; these don't hit Zillow alerts.

programs.name: Opendoor × Redfin offer integration
programs.what: Cash-offer requests via Redfin's apps (live in Phoenix/Atlanta, expanding). Relevant later as a SELL-side tool for Carolina Orchards.

programs.name: Redfin Hot Homes flag
programs.what: Algorithmic likely-to-sell-fast flag — useful inverted: townhomes WITHOUT the flag + 30+ days on market are your negotiation pool.
programs.action: The daily scan already catches Redfin alerts; filter for stale non-hot listings.

programs.name: Realtor.com new-construction hub
programs.what: Builder-fed inventory incl. quick move-in filters; often surfaces builder incentives portals miss.
programs.action: Covered by existing scraper; check quick-move-in filter manually at quarter-end.

programs.name: Builder quarter-end quick-move-in pushes
programs.what: The single most reliable 'special deal' in the data: written flexibility appears in the last ~10 days of each quarter (proven 6/22 in your own inbox).
programs.action: Next window Sep 22–30 — pre-staged offers ready.

programs.name: HomeScout / 'Scout' apps
programs.what: White-label MLS search apps (agent-sponsored) with direct-MLS freshness — sometimes faster than portals. UNVERIFIED which 'Scout' you meant; if it's a specific app, name it and it gets folded in.
programs.action: Ask Tracy for her brokerage's HomeScout-style app for raw-MLS speed.

apis.name: RentCast
apis.powers: Rent estimates + comps per address — would replace the app's $/sqft rent model with address-level AVM rents

apis.name: HUD Fair Market Rents API
apis.powers: Official FMR by zip — sanity-check floor for rent assumptions

apis.name: US Census ACS
apis.powers: Tract-level income/values/growth — feeds the Innovation Index equity metric

apis.name: Charlotte Open Data Portal
apis.powers: Building permits + zoning variances via ArcGIS REST — your leading-indicator signal (permit spikes precede appreciation)

apis.name: Mecklenburg POLARIS / Assessor
apis.powers: Parcel data, tax values, sale history — the excise-stamp price trick, automated

apis.name: ATTOM Data
apis.powers: The HouseCanary-class option: AVMs, sales comps, foreclosure, school data nationally

apis.name: Zillow Bridge / MLS Grid
apis.powers: Real MLS-grade listing feeds — requires broker relationship (Tracy's brokerage could sponsor IDX access)

apis.name: RapidAPI Zillow/Realtor wrappers
apis.powers: Unofficial listing pulls — works but violates portal ToS; fine for personal use, NOT for a paid product

apis.name: HomeHarvest (open source)
apis.powers: Python scraper for Realtor.com/Zillow/Redfin — same ToS caveat; personal-use replacement for the blocked scrapers

api_catalog.recommendation: Wire the four free ones (RentCast free tier, HUD, Census, Charlotte Open Data) — they're legally clean, cover rent + fundamentals + leading indicators, and cost $0. ATTOM/MLS Grid only if the app becomes a paid product.

monetization.verdict: Viable as a niche paid tool, but the moat is the ANALYSIS + curated builder intel, not the listing data (reselling scraped portal data violates ToS and MLS licensing — that's the line to never cross).

tiers.gets: Public Pages site: listings browser, market model, submarket table
Daily scan alerts, investment scores + fit scores, builder KB with risk flags, negotiation playbooks, leaseback program directory
Per-address underwriting (RentCast comps + POLARIS tax history + deed chain), pre-staged offer packs, quarter-end deal windows

path: 1. Move backend to a $5/mo VPS or Fly.io (Pages stays as free tier)
2. Stripe Payment Links + simple magic-link auth (no password infra)
3. Replace scraped portal data with licensed/free sources before charging (RentCast+HUD+Census+Open Data are clean)
4. The unique sellable asset: builder risk flags + leaseback program intel — nobody aggregates that
monetization.legal_caution: MLS data licensing and portal ToS prohibit reselling their listing data. Monetize your analysis, scores, and curated intel — never raw scraped listings.

## Snapshot

## Evidence-backed findings

| Claim or observation | Type | Evidence/citation | Confidence | As of |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Operates in 19 markets, 13 states; 125,000+ homes built since founding in 1976 | Fact (company claim) | SRC-004 | Medium — company-published, not independently audited | 2026-07-04 |
| Buyer-review aggregate rating 2.9/5 (3 reviews, 44 complaints) | Opinion aggregate | SRC-003 | Low — very small review sample | 2026-07-04 |
| Employee-review rating 4.4/5 across 595+ reviews; 89% would recommend employer | Opinion aggregate | SRC-002 | Medium — larger sample, but employee sentiment ≠ buyer sentiment | 2026-07-04 |
| Recurring buyer complaints: warranty limitations, craftsmanship, communication gaps | Opinion aggregate | SRC-001, SRC-003 | Medium | 2026-07-04 |
| Model-home sales carry only the warranty balance from original CO date, not a fresh 1-year term | Assumption (pattern inferred from Tri Pointe's disclosed practice; not independently confirmed as a David Weekley-wide policy) | SRC-027 (analogous Tri Pointe disclosure), buyer's own contract terms | Low — inferred by analogy, not confirmed for David Weekley specifically | 2026-07-04 |

## Questions, risks, and next actions

- The gap between weak buyer reviews (2.9/5) and strong employee reviews (4.4/5) suggests inconsistent field/subcontractor execution rather than a management problem — worth probing directly with the sales rep about which subcontractors serve this specific community.
- Confirm in writing whether the model-home warranty truly starts from original CO date or from the buyer's closing date — this profile currently treats that as an unconfirmed assumption, not a verified fact.
- A second model in this builder's Charlotte portfolio was referenced informally (buyer's own inbox, not a public source) — treat as a lead requiring direct confirmation, not a verified fact.

- The energy-efficiency claims (M.R.E.S., HERS mid-50s–60s) are a genuine potential differentiator for a rental-yield calculation (lower utility costs can support higher achievable rent or better tenant retention) — but they rest on self-reported homeowner data in this pass, not an independent HERS audit. Request the actual HERS certificate for any specific home before relying on this in an investment model.
- Get an independent inspection regardless of the energy-efficiency marketing, given the repeated quality/warranty complaint pattern.

# Mungo Homes

> Research status: Reputation research complete via secondary sources (2026-07-05). No first-person contact yet — Nichols Landing (NW Charlotte) is the actionable lead requiring a site visit.

## Scope

- **Relevant market/community:** Charlotte, NC — Nichols Landing (NW Charlotte) and Lakeview Village townhome communities
- **Profile updated:** 2026-07-05
- **Research owner:** Claude research pass (secondary sources only; no first-person evidence yet)

## Snapshot

| Field | Information |
|---|---|
| Relevant communities | Nichols Landing (Northwest Charlotte — ~300 townhomes, 1,471–1,697 sqft, in the buyer's triangulated 28216 upside corridor); Lakeview Village (41-homesite townhome community, 1,456–1,530 sqft, 3bd/2.5ba, 1-car garage) |
| Product and price range | Townhomes at the affordable end; Charlotte pricing to be confirmed on site visit (Columbia-market equivalents run high $200s–$300s) |
| Contract structure | Standard new-construction purchase; part of Clayton Properties Group (Berkshire Hathaway) since 2018, family-founded 1954, Irmo SC |
| Incentives | Unknown for Charlotte communities — ask; Clayton-affiliated builders commonly tie incentives to affiliated lending |
| Warranty and service | Recurring complaint theme: warranty claims validated on visit but unaddressed afterward; partial completions then denial after warranty expiry |

## Evidence-backed findings

| Claim or observation | Type | Evidence/citation | Confidence | As of |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Family-founded 1954 (Irmo, SC); part of Clayton Properties Group (Berkshire Hathaway) since 2018; Steven Mungo remains Executive Chairman | Fact (company claim) | SRC-055 | Medium — company-published | 2026-07-05 |
| Nichols Landing is Mungo's newest Charlotte townhome community: ~300 homes, 1,471–1,697 sqft, Northwest Charlotte | Fact (company claim) | SRC-055 | Medium — company-published; location places it in the buyer's 28216/28214 momentum corridor | 2026-07-05 |
| Buyer-review aggregate 1.6/5 across 25 reviews (PissedConsumer); Yelp similarly dissatisfied | Opinion aggregate | SRC-056 | Low–Medium — small sample, complaint-biased platform | 2026-07-05 |
| Recurring complaints: flooring replaced multiple times, unresolved warranty claims (HVAC, roofing, plumbing, drainage), builder attention shifting to new communities before finishing established ones | Opinion aggregate | SRC-057, SRC-058 | Medium — consistent theme across BBB complaints and ComplaintsBoard | 2026-07-05 |
| Employee rating 3.5/5 across 49 Glassdoor reviews; only 53% would recommend as employer | Opinion aggregate | SRC-059 | Medium — lowest employee-recommend rate among profiled builders, weak internal-culture signal | 2026-07-05 |
| Berkshire/Clayton ownership provides balance-sheet stability but has not translated into strong buyer-review outcomes | Assumption (synthesis of SRC-055 vs SRC-056/057) | SRC-055, SRC-056, SRC-057 | Low — inference, not measured | 2026-07-05 |

## Questions, risks, and next actions

- **Strengths:** Berkshire/Clayton balance-sheet stability; large multi-phase Charlotte townhome pipeline (Nichols Landing ~300 homes) in the buyer's highest-conviction corridor; 70-year family-founded operating history.
- **Nichols Landing is the action item**: ~300 townhomes in the 28216-adjacent corridor is a large, multi-phase community — that means model homes on site for years and multiple closeout windows. Visit, get pricing, and ask when the first model releases and whether Mungo does leasebacks (no public evidence either way).
- The warranty-complaint pattern (validated-then-ignored claims) is the sharpest risk signal here — worse in character than volume. Any Mungo offer should carry the same protections as the Tri Pointe playbook: independent inspection, written warranty response timelines, and escrowed punch-list items.
- 53% employee-recommend is the weakest internal signal of any profiled builder — probe subcontractor consistency for the specific community.
- Pricing gap: no Charlotte-specific pricing evidence yet; treat any "from the $290s"-style marketing as unverified until quoted in writing.

profiles.title: Tri Pointe Homes
profiles.content:
# Tri Pointe Homes

> Research status: Reputation research complete via secondary sources; deal-specific terms are first-person evidence from the buyer's own inbox (2026-07-04).

## Snapshot

| Field | Information |
|---|---|
| Relevant communities | Archer Row (Waxhaw, NC), Grahym at Southbridge & Camdyn at Southbridge (Fort Mill, SC), Context at Oakhurst (Charlotte 28205) |
| Product and price range | Archer Row final release $439,990–$515,682 (buyer's inbox, first-person); decorated "Beau" model (Lot 1, 7937 Cedarsmith Ct) $479,990 with full furniture (confirmed in writing, first-person) |
| Contract structure | Model-home sale; Old Republic 1-yr third-party warranty + structural-warranty remainder in lieu of standard 1/2/10 (buyer's inbox, first-person) |
| Incentives | Southbridge move-in-ready: $20K flex, either $10K price + $10K closing, or up to $20K closing (buyer's inbox, first-person). Archer Row: $10K–$12.5K closing costs tied to TPC + move-in package on non-decorated units (buyer's inbox, first-person) |
| Warranty and service | Model-home sales specifically excluded from standard 1/2/10; 1-yr Old Republic + structural remainder only (buyer's inbox, first-person — Archer Row) |

## Evidence-backed findings

| Claim or observation | Type | Evidence/citation | Confidence | As of |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Nationally rated #2 in "America's Most Trusted" rankings (per prior research session; not re-verified against a primary Lifestory Research source in this pass) | Fact (unverified in this pass) | Carried over from prior research, no new source located this pass | Low — not re-confirmed against a primary source | 2026-07-04 |
| Local Southbridge reviews average 1.15/5 across 26 reviews | Opinion aggregate | SRC-028 (buyer's own prior Drive research) | Low — original review-platform citation not independently re-derived in this pass; treat as a lead, not a confirmed statistic | 2026-07-04 |
| ComplaintsBoard aggregate: 3.7/5, 13 complaints, 76% resolved / 24% unresolved as of 2026-05-27 | Opinion aggregate | SRC-024 | Medium | 2026-05-27 |
| Documented construction-quality complaints: foundation issues, cheap workmanship, minimal grading/site-work oversight, unfinished punch-list items | Opinion aggregate | SRC-023, SRC-025 | Medium | 2026-07-04 |
| Colorado-specific construction-defect litigation exists against this builder | Fact (legal filings referenced by a law firm's marketing content) | SRC-026 | Low — source is a plaintiff's-side law firm, inherently advocacy-oriented; not a neutral court record review | 2026-07-04 |
| Model-home sales exclude standard 1/2/10 warranty (Archer Row specifically) | Fact (first-person, direct rep disclosure) | SRC-027 | High — direct written disclosure from the builder's own sales rep | 2026-06-20 |
| Taylor Shane (rep) invited a custom negotiation scenario in writing | Fact (first-person) | SRC-027 | High | 2026-06-20 |
| The gap between the #2 national trust claim and 1.15/5 local reviews is the largest reputation-execution gap in this entire research set | Opinion (comparative synthesis, this researcher's own conclusion) | Derived from SRC-024, SRC-028, and comparison against the other 11 builder profiles | Low — both anchor figures (#2 trust rank, 1.15/5) are themselves unverified-in-this-pass; the "gap" conclusion inherits that weakness | 2026-07-04 |

## Questions, risks, and next actions

- **Highest-priority open item:** re-verify both the "#2 national trust" claim and the "1.15/5 local reviews" figure against primary sources before treating the "reputation-execution gap" as established fact — both currently rest on prior-session research, not sources re-confirmed today.
- Independent inspection and written warranty terms should be treated as non-negotiable preconditions for any Tri Pointe offer, given the volume and specificity of construction-quality complaints (SRC-023, SRC-025) even setting the unverified 1.15/5 figure aside.
- Get the model-home warranty terms (1-yr Old Republic + structural remainder) confirmed in writing for Southbridge and Oakhurst specifically — the current evidence for this term is Archer Row-specific (SRC-027) and may not be identical at other Tri Pointe Charlotte communities.
- Incentive structures have shifted mid-negotiation before at this builder (Oakhurst, 2026-06-15) — get final numbers in writing before committing to a specific unit.

| 6 | Tri Pointe Homes | 14 | Construction + warranty scored; extensive first-person deal data | Most model-sale inventory + only written custom-scenario offer | Reputation-execution gap (unverified 1.15/5 local figure) + no 1/2/10 on model sales | [tri-pointe-homes.md](builders/tri-pointe-homes.md) |
| 6 | Meritage Homes | 14 | Construction + warranty scored | Genuine energy-efficiency differentiator (M.R.E.S.) | "Warranty is a joke" repeated verbatim across reviews | [meritage-homes.md](builders/meritage-homes.md) |

| 11 | D.R. Horton | 10 | Construction + warranty scored | Largest model-sale inventory (most communities) | Lowest buyer-satisfaction scores (1.3–1.7/5) of any builder researched | [dr-horton.md](builders/dr-horton.md) |
| — | Taylor Morrison | Insufficient evidence to score | No complaint-platform data located in this pass (gap, not a clean bill of health) | #1 America's Most Trusted, 11 years running; marketed leaseback program | No buyer-complaint data found — construction/warranty dimensions unscored | [taylor-morrison.md](builders/taylor-morrison.md) |

## Method

- Score against the weight definitions in [Real-Estate-Knowledge/01-Buying-Criteria.md](https://github.com/bitflipper1/Real-Estate-Knowledge/blob/8fbc132f201ec5bab37adf2d819b394ca09e7187/01-Buying-Criteria.md).
- Evidence cited in each builder profile under [`builders/`](builders/); full source list in [`sources/source-index.md`](sources/source-index.md).
- This pass distinguishes local-division performance from national reputation where evidence allowed (notably Tri Pointe, Mattamy, Eastwood, Smith Douglas — all have Charlotte/regional-specific data; the rest are national-aggregate only).
- Sample size and date range: all review-platform data gathered 2026-07-04 in a single pass; no historical trend data collected. Complaint counts and star ratings are noted per-builder in each profile rather than repeated here.
- Taylor Morrison is listed last/unranked rather than assumed best — its strong trust-award signal is real but this pass did not locate the buyer-complaint-platform data used to score every other builder, so it isn't comparable on the same basis.

## Source status

- **Primary:** original contract, official disclosure, lender quote, tax record, or first-party document (includes company newsrooms/investor relations and regulatory filings here).
- **Secondary:** reputable analysis, reporting, or aggregate review platform that interprets or aggregates primary information.
- **First-person:** buyer, agent, salesperson, homeowner, or chat account; useful but perspective-dependent.
- **Lead only:** uncited statement requiring verification.

## Known limitations of this source set

- All SRC-001 through SRC-054 entries (2026-07-04 pass) and SRC-055 through SRC-059 (Mungo Homes, 2026-07-05 pass) are aggregate review-platform data gathered in a single 2026-07-04 web-research pass — none are primary contracts, lender worksheets, or tax records specific to a Charlotte transaction. Per the evidence standard, treat every builder profile's findings as **reputation signal**, not verified fact about any specific home or community.
- Review-platform ratings (BBB, ComplaintsBoard, ConsumerAffairs, Trustpilot, Yelp, Birdeye) carry inherent selection bias in both directions — dissatisfied customers are more likely to post complaints; builder-published review pages (SRC-004, SRC-041, SRC-054) are curated and carry positive-selection bias. Treat all star ratings as directional, not statistically representative.
- SRC-028's 1.15/5 Southbridge figure originates from the buyer's own prior Drive research and was not independently re-derived or re-verified against the original review platform in this pass.

## Note on source-repo access

`bitflipper1/Real-Estate-Knowledge` was private and outside this app's GitHub session scope for most of this research pass; access opened up mid-pass via an anonymous `git clone`, which is read-only. These profiles were authored to match that repo's exact structure and evidence discipline (Snapshot table → Evidence-backed findings with source IDs, claim type, confidence, and as-of dates → Questions/risks → Sources), but the underlying research here is Charlotte-market secondary-source review data (BBB, ComplaintsBoard, ConsumerAffairs, etc.), not the primary-document evidence (contracts, lender worksheets) that repo's standard calls for. Nothing here has been pushed to that repo — this app has no write credentials to it.
